<path>/home/chris/develop/open_source/sphinx-need-sysml/specs/002-full-sysml-diagrams/research.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Research: Full SysML v2 Diagram Coverage
2: 
3: **Date**: 2026-05-20
4: **Feature**: specs/002-full-sysml-diagrams
5: 
6: All "NEEDS CLARIFICATION" markers were resolved during `/speckit-clarify` (see [spec.md § Clarifications](spec.md#clarifications)). This document records the technical research that informs the implementation plan.
7: 
8: ---
9: 
10: ## 1. PlantUML native diagram modes per SysML diagram
11: 
12: ### Decision
13: For each of the six PlantUML-routed diagram directives, the directive body uses PlantUML's native diagram mode (not the class-diagram approximation that `needsysml-bdd` uses). PlantUML's syntax is wrapped inside `@startuml`/`@enduml` by `needuml`; the `:config: sysml_<diagram>` line supplies the skinparam styling.
14: 
15: ### Rationale
16: PlantUML's native modes give richer fidelity than reusing the class-diagram engine for every SysML diagram:
17: 
18: - **State machine** — first-class `state X`, nested `state X { state Y }`, transitions `X --> Y : trigger [guard] / effect`, history pseudostates `[H]` / `[H*]`, choice via `state x <<choice>>`, junction via `state x <<junction>>`.
19: - **Activity** — beta activity syntax: `start`, `:Action;`, `if (cond?) then (yes) … else (no) endif`, `fork`, `fork again`, `end fork`, `partition "Lane" { … }` for swimlanes, `:Action| object;` for object flows.
20: - **Sequence** — `participant A`, `A -> B : msg` (sync), `A ->> B : msg` (async), `A --> B : return`, combined fragments: `alt`/`else`/`end`, `opt`, `loop`, `par`/`else`, `break`, `critical`.
21: - **Use case** — `actor :A:`, `usecase (UC1) as UC1`, `:A: --> UC1`, `UC1 .> UC2 : <<include>>`, `UC1 .> UC2 : <<extend>>`, `rectangle "SystemName" { … }` for the system boundary.
22: - **Package** — `package "Name" { }`, nesting allowed, dependencies `pkgA ..> pkgB : <<import>>` (or `<<use>>` / `<<realize>>`).
23: - **Parametric** — falls back to the class-diagram engine: `class "fuel_eq" <<constraint>> { expression }` with binding connectors `param ..> value_prop : "kW"`. Acknowledged in docs as an approximation.
24: 
25: ### Alternatives considered
26: - **Reuse class-diagram syntax for every diagram** — uniform but ugly for activities and sequences. Rejected because PlantUML state/activity/sequence/use case modes are well-supported and produce idiomatic output.
27: - **Compile to Mermaid** — Mermaid is a Sphinx-needs option for some users but doesn't cover sequence combined fragments well and has no parametric story. Rejected.
28: - **Bring in a separate UML library** (e.g. `plantweb`) — adds an external service dependency. Rejected; sphinx-needs already wraps `sphinxcontrib-plantuml`.
29: 
30: ### Code patterns
31: 
32: **State machine** (sysml_stm):
33: ```plantuml
34: @startuml
35: [*] --> Off
36: state Off
37: state Starting
38: state Running {
39:     [*] --> Idle
40:     Idle --> Loaded : load_request
41:     Loaded --> Idle : unload_request
42:     [H] -> Idle
43: }
44: state Stopping
45: Off --> Starting : key_on / start_seq
46: Starting --> Running : engine_ok
47: Running --> Stopping : key_off
48: Stopping --> Off : spindown_complete
49: Running --> Off : fault [severity=='critical']
50: @enduml
51: ```
52: 
53: **Activity** (sysml_act):
54: ```plantuml
55: @startuml
56: |Driver|
57: start
58: :Insert key;
59: :Turn key;
60: |ECU|
61: :Power on rails;
62: fork
63:     :Crank starter;
64: fork again
65:     :Fuel pump prime;
66: end fork
67: if (engine_ok?) then (yes)
68:     :Run engine;
69: else (no)
70:     :Show fault;
71: endif
72: stop
73: @enduml
74: ```
75: 
76: **Sequence** (sysml_sd):
77: ```plantuml
78: @startuml
79: participant Driver
80: participant ECU
81: participant Starter
82: Driver -> ECU : turnKey()
83: activate ECU
84: alt key_position == "start"
85:     ECU ->> Starter : crank()
86:     Starter --> ECU : ok
87: else
88:     ECU --> Driver : noop
89: end
90: loop while engine_warming
91:     ECU ->> ECU : adjust_fuel()
92: end
93: deactivate ECU
94: @enduml
95: ```
96: 
97: **Use case** (sysml_uc):
98: ```plantuml
99: @startuml
100: left to right direction
101: :Driver:
102: :Mechanic:
103: rectangle "Vehicle" {
104:     usecase (Start engine) as UC1
105:     usecase (Diagnose fault) as UC2
106:     usecase (Authenticate key) as UC3
107:     UC1 .> UC3 : <<include>>
108:     UC2 .> UC1 : <<extend>>
109: }
110: :Driver: --> UC1
111: :Mechanic: --> UC2
112: @enduml
113: ```
114: 
115: **Package** (sysml_pkg):
116: ```plantuml
117: @startuml
118: package "VehicleSystem" {
119:     package "Powertrain" {
120:         package "Engine" { }
121:         package "Transmission" { }
122:     }
123:     package "Chassis" { }
124: }
125: Powertrain ..> Chassis : <<use>>
126: @enduml
127: ```
128: 
129: **Parametric** (sysml_par — class diagram approximation):
130: ```plantuml
131: @startuml
132: skinparam class<<constraint>> { BackgroundColor #FFF0DC }
133: class "FuelConsumption" <<constraint>> {
134:     fuel = output * duration / efficiency
135:     --
136:     + output : ConstraintParameter
137:     + duration : ConstraintParameter
138:     + efficiency : ConstraintParameter
139:     + fuel : ConstraintParameter
140: }
141: "engine.output" --> "FuelConsumption::output" : "kW"
142: "drive.duration" --> "FuelConsumption::duration" : "s"
143: "engine.efficiency" --> "FuelConsumption::efficiency" : ""
144: "FuelConsumption::fuel" --> "trip.fuel_used" : "L"
145: @enduml
146: ```
147: 
148: ---
149: 
150: ## 2. sphinx-need-svg deferred-rendering pattern
151: 
152: ### Decision
153: Every new `-svg` companion directive registers a `sphinx_need_svg.directives.needsvg.Needsvg` placeholder node and stores its template body in `env.needsvg_all_data[targetid]`. Rendering happens later in `process_needsvg` (connected to `doctree-resolved`). The new directives **do not** call `SphinxNeedsData.get_needs_view()` themselves.
154: 
155: ### Rationale
156: The currently-fixed `needsysml-bdd-svg` directive used to call `render_jinja_svg` inside its own `run()` method, which transitively called `SphinxNeedsData(env).get_needs_view()` during doctree-read. That froze the needs registry — every subsequent `partdef`/`part`/etc. directive on the same page failed with `Needs have already been post-processed and frozen`. The fix is to defer rendering until `doctree-resolved` (after all needs are collected). The existing `sphinx_need_svg.directives.needsvg` module already implements that pattern, so the SVG variants reuse it.
157: 
158: ### Pattern (canonical, from `needsysml_svg.py`)
159: 
160: ```python
161: from sphinx_need_svg.directives.needsvg import Needsvg
162: from docutils import nodes
163: 
164: class NeedsymlXxxSvgDirective(SphinxDirective):
165:     def run(self) -> list[Any]:
166:         # Build template by string-substituting the root arg and option values
167:         # into the canonical svg template for this diagram type.
168:         content = _SVG_TEMPLATE_FOR_THIS_DIAGRAM \
169:             .replace("__ROOT_ID__", self.arguments[0]) \
170:             .replace("__FILTER_EXPR__", filter_expr.replace('"', '\\"'))
171: 
172:         env = self.env
173:         targetid = f"needsvg-{env.docname}-{env.new_serialno('needsvg')}"
174:         env.needsvg_all_data = getattr(env, "needsvg_all_data", {})
175:         env.needsvg_all_data[targetid] = {
176:             "docname": env.docname,
177:             "lineno": self.lineno,
178:             "content": content,
179:             "options": {
180:                 "width": self.options.get("width", "100%"),
181:                 "height": "auto",
182:                 "align": self.options.get("align", "center"),
183:                 "debug": False,
184:             },
185:         }
186:         targetnode = nodes.target("", "", ids=[targetid])
187:         node = Needsvg("")
188:         node["targetid"] = targetid
189:         return [targetnode, node]
190: ```
191: 
192: ### Alternatives considered
193: - **Custom placeholder + own doctree-resolved handler** per directive — duplicates `process_needsvg` six times. Rejected.
194: - **Eager rendering with manual `app._needs_registry_frozen` reset** — fights the framework. Rejected.
195: 
196: ---
197: 
198: ## 3. Pseudostate notation
199: 
200: ### Decision
201: PlantUML state machine syntax supports every pseudostate kind chosen in spec clarification Q2:
202: 
203: | Pseudo kind | PlantUML syntax |
204: |---|---|
205: | `initial` | `[*] --> NextState` |
206: | `final` | `NextState --> [*]` |
207: | `shallowHistory` | `state hist <<history>>` then `hist --> Foo` |
208: | `deepHistory` | `state hist <<history*>>` then `hist --> Foo` |
209: | `choice` | `state c <<choice>>` then `[*] --> c`, `c --> A : [g==1]`, `c --> B : [g==2]` |
210: | `junction` | `state j <<junction>>` (same wiring as choice; junction is a static merge, choice is dynamic) |
211: 
212: Initial and final are already covered by existing `is_initial` / `is_final` boolean fields, but the renderer must translate them into the `[*]` syntax when emitting transitions. History/choice/junction need a new `pseudo_kind` field.
213: 
214: ### Rationale
215: The chosen set (Q2 = Option B) matches what PlantUML can express natively — no custom rendering required. Pseudostates are represented as kinds of state, not separate need types, matching SysML v2's metamodel.
216: 
217: ### Field design
218: Add `pseudo_kind` to `SYSML_FIELDS`:
219: ```python
220: {
221:     "name": "pseudo_kind",
222:     "description": "Pseudostate kind: initial / final / shallowHistory / deepHistory / choice / junction",
223:     "schema": {"type": "string", "enum": [
224:         "initial", "final", "shallowHistory", "deepHistory", "choice", "junction"
225:     ]},
226: }
227: ```
228: 
229: ### Alternatives considered
230: - **Six new need types** (`InitialPseudostate`, `FinalPseudostate`, …) — bloats the type registry; pseudostates are conceptually states, not first-class elements. Rejected.
231: - **Boolean flags per kind** (`is_choice`, `is_history`, …) — six booleans where one enum suffices. Rejected.
232: 
233: ---
234: 
235: ## 4. Swimlane (activity partition) syntax
236: 
237: ### Decision
238: Use PlantUML activity-beta `partition "Name" { … }` blocks. The activity directive's Jinja template iterates over distinct values of a new `partition` field on actions and emits one partition block per unique value.
239: 
240: ### Rationale
241: PlantUML's activity beta syntax has first-class swimlanes. Native swimlanes also render correctly in PDF when the PlantUML output format is `svg`.
242: 
243: ### Template skeleton
244: ```jinja2
245: @startuml
246: {% set partitions = filter("type == 'Action' and definition == '" + activity_id + "'")
247:                     | groupby("partition") %}
248: {% for partition_name, actions in partitions %}
249:     {% if partition_name %}
250:         |{{ partition_name }}|
251:     {% endif %}
252:     {% for action in actions %}
253:         :{{ action.title }};
254:     {% endfor %}
255: {% endfor %}
256: {% for cf in filter("type == 'ControlFlow' and " + activity_id_match) %}
257:     {{ cf.from_action }} --> {{ cf.to_action }}
258: {% endfor %}
259: @enduml
260: ```
261: 
262: ### Field design
263: Add `partition` (string) to `SYSML_FIELDS`. Optional — actions with no partition render at the top level.
264: 
265: ---
266: 
267: ## 5. Combined fragments (sequence diagrams)
268: 
269: ### Decision
270: Each message belongs to at most one fragment, identified by the `fragment` field (pointing to a `MessageFragment` instance) and a `fragment_kind` enum. PlantUML emits the fragment frame around the contained messages.
271: 
272: ### Rationale
273: SysML/UML supports all six combined-fragment kinds enumerated in spec FR-006 (`alt`, `opt`, `loop`, `par`, `neg`, `critical`). PlantUML natively renders all of them. Modeling fragments as elements (rather than implicit ranges) lets us list and link to them like any other need.
274: 
275: ### Field design
276: - `fragment_kind`: enum string (`alt` / `opt` / `loop` / `par` / `neg` / `critical`) on `Message`.
277: - `fragment_guard`: optional string (the guard expression printed in the frame header).
278: - For multi-message fragments: messages share the same `fragment` group via a `fragment_group` string. The renderer wraps any group of messages with a common `fragment_group` in a single PlantUML fragment block.
279: 
280: ### Alternatives considered
281: - **A separate `MessageFragment` need type** — more explicit but introduces extra cross-references. Reserved for a future enhancement; v1 uses fragment_group/fragment_kind on the messages themselves.
282: 
283: ---
284: 
285: ## 6. Use case extends / includes / generalizes
286: 
287: ### Decision
288: Three string fields on `UseCase`: `extends`, `includes`, `generalizes`, each holding a comma-separated list of `USECASE-NNN` IDs (matching the existing comma-list convention for `satisfies` / `refines` / `allocates`).
289: 
290: ### PlantUML output
291: - `extends` → dashed `..>` from the extending UC to the extended UC, labelled `<<extend>>`.
292: - `includes` → dashed `..>` from the including UC to the included UC, labelled `<<include>>`.
293: - `generalizes` → solid arrow `-->` with hollow triangle head from child to parent. PlantUML's syntax for that is `<|--`.
294: 
295: ### Subject (system boundary)
296: `UseCase.subject` is a string holding the system-boundary name (free text, not a need ID). The renderer wraps all use cases that share the same `subject` value inside one `rectangle "subject" { … }`. Multiple subjects on one diagram are allowed.
297: 
298: ### Rationale
299: String fields match the existing comma-list convention so engineers don't need a new mental model. Subject is free-text rather than a need ID because a "system boundary" in UC diagrams is a label, not a separately-modeled entity in this extension.
300: 
301: ---
302: 
303: ## 7. Allocation matrix renderer
304: 
305: ### Decision
306: The matrix directive emits a `docutils.nodes.table` with row headers = needs matching `:rows:` filter, column headers = needs matching `:columns:` filter, and a marker (✓ in HTML, "X" in plain-text outputs) at intersections where the row need's `allocates` field references the column need's ID. Defaults per spec Q3 are `:rows:` = needs with `allocates != ""`, `:columns:` = needs referenced by any `allocates` value.
307: 
308: ### Rationale
309: A docutils table picks up the active Sphinx theme's table styling (immaterial, alabaster, etc.) and renders identically in HTML, PDF, and ePub. Unlike SVG diagrams, the matrix is queryable in non-HTML outputs.
310: 
311: ### Pseudocode
312: ```python
313: def run(self):
314:     rows_filter = self.options.get("rows", 'allocates != ""')
315:     cols_filter = self.options.get("columns", _DEFAULT_COLS_FILTER)
316:     rows = filter_needs_view(needs, config, rows_filter)
317:     cols = sorted({need.id for r in rows for need in r.allocates.split(",")})
318:     cols_filtered = [c for c in cols if c in needs and _match(needs[c], cols_filter)]
319:     table = nodes.table(...)
320:     # header row: empty cell, then col-need refs
321:     # data rows: row-need ref, then ✓ / ∅ markers per col
322:     return [table]
323: ```
324: 
325: ### Alternatives considered
326: - **SVG matrix** — overkill; tables already exist as a first-class docutils node. Rejected.
327: - **Reuse `needtable`** — `needtable` does not natively support a row-vs-column matrix layout; it produces one row per need. Rejected (but the implementation can borrow filter parsing from `needtable`).
328: 
329: ---
330: 
331: ## 8. Test fixture strategy
332: 
333: ### Decision
334: Each new directive gets its own pytest module + `tests/doc_test/<directive>/` fixture (one `conf.py` + one `index.rst`). Fixtures are minimal: one `statedef` + three states + three transitions for `test_stm_directive.py`, etc. Smoke tests assert (a) `app.build()` finishes with no unexpected warnings, (b) at least one fixture-defined need ID appears in the rendered doctree.
335: 
336: ### Rationale
337: Matches the existing convention used by `test_bdd_directive.py` / `test_ibd_directive.py` / `test_req_directive.py`. Per-directive fixtures keep failures isolated — when `test_stm_directive.py` fails it doesn't drag down the sequence-diagram fixture.
338: 
339: ### One-shot smoke pattern
340: ```python
341: import pytest
342: from sphinx.testing.fixtures import make_app, shared_result
343: 
344: @pytest.fixture
345: def stm_app(make_app, tmp_path):
346:     # copy doc_test/stm to tmp_path, build with html, return app
347:     ...
348: 
349: def test_stm_builds_without_error(stm_app):
350:     stm_app.build()
351:     assert not stm_app.statuscode
352: 
353: def test_stm_renders_fixture_state(stm_app):
354:     stm_app.build()
355:     text = stm_app.env.get_doctree("index").astext()
356:     assert "SD-001" in text or "Off" in text
357: ```
358: 
359: ### Alternatives considered
360: - **One mega-fixture used by every test** — couples failure modes; if one test corrupts the build env, others cascade. Rejected.
361: 
362: ---
363: 
364: ## 9. Two-phase release packaging
365: 
366: ### Decision
367: - v1 (`sphinxcontrib-sysml 0.3.0`): User Stories 1–5 — `needsysml-stm`, `needsysml-act`, `needsysml-sd`, `needsysml-uc`, `needsysml-pkg` (each with `-svg` companion).
368: - v1.1 (`sphinxcontrib-sysml 0.4.0`): User Stories 6–7 — `needsysml-alloc` (matrix), `needsysml-par` (parametric, with `-svg` companion).
369: 
370: All thirteen new need types and ~22 fields ship in v1, even those used only by v1.1 directives. This keeps downstream user models stable across the two releases.
371: 
372: ### Rationale
373: - Spec clarification Q5 = Option C.
374: - Registering the v1.1-only types in v1 means engineers using v1 can already declare `ConstraintBlock` / `ValueProperty` / `BindingConnector` in their models (queryable via tables) even before the parametric diagram directive exists. When v1.1 lands they just add `..needsysml-par::` directives without re-registering anything.
375: 
376: ### Alternatives considered
377: - **Defer v1.1 element types to v1.1** — would force a data-model migration when v1.1 ships. Rejected because the registration cost is near-zero.
378: - **Single big-bang release** — rejected by user during clarification.
379: 
380: ---
381: 
382: ## 10. Backwards-compatibility scan
383: 
384: ### Findings
385: The new feature has zero conflicts with the existing 001 feature's surface:
386: 
387: - No existing type names are reused. The 13 new prefixes (`TRANS-`, `CTRLFLOW-`, `OBJFLOW-`, `PKG-`, `DEP-`, `USECASE-`, `ACTOR-`, `CONSTRAINT-`, `PARAM-`, `VALUE-`, `BIND-`, `LIFELINE-`, `MSG-`) do not collide with the 14 existing prefixes (`PD-`, `P-`, `POD-`, `PO-`, `IFD-`, `IF-`, `CD-`, `C-`, `AD-`, `A-`, `SD-`, `SU-`, `RD-`, `R-`).
388: - `pseudo_kind` is a new field; existing `is_initial` / `is_final` continue to work and are still honored by the renderer.
389: - `needsysml-req`'s positional filter argument stays valid; the new `:filter:` option only fires when present.
390: - The existing `needsysml-bdd-svg` directive remains unchanged.
391: 
392: ### Pre-existing test failure (out of scope)
393: `tests/test_req_directive.py::TestReqDirective::test_req_filter_expression_respected` is failing on `main` before this feature begins (see git stash test in the work session). It is **not** caused by this feature and **not** in scope to fix here. The plan does not regress it further; success criterion SC-007 measures regression beyond that baseline.

metadata.preview:
# Research: Full SysML v2 Diagram Coverage

**Date**: 2026-05-20
**Feature**: specs/002-full-sysml-diagrams

All "NEEDS CLARIFICATION" markers were resolved during `/speckit-clarify` (see [spec.md § Clarifications](spec.md#clarifications)). This document records the technical research that informs the implementation plan.

---

## 1. PlantUML native diagram modes per SysML diagram

### Decision
For each of the six PlantUML-routed diagram directives, the directive body uses PlantUML's native diagram mode (not the class-diagram approximation that `needsysml-bdd` uses). PlantUML's syntax is wrapped inside `@startuml`/`@enduml` by `needuml`; the `:config: sysml_<diagram>` line supplies the skinparam styling.

### Rationale
PlantUML's native modes give richer fidelity than reusing the class-diagram engine for every SysML diagram:

- **State machine** — first-class `state X`, nested `state X { state Y }`, transitions `X --> Y : trigger [guard] / effect`, history pseudostates `[H]` / `[H*]`, choice via `state x <<choice>>`, junction via `state x <<junction>>`.
- **Activity** — beta activity syntax: `start`, `:Action;`, `if (cond?) then (yes) … else (no) endif`, `fork`, `fork again`, `end fork`, `partition "Lane" { … }` for swimlanes, `:Action| object;` for object flows.
- **Sequence** — `participant A`, `A -> B : msg` (sync), `A ->> B : msg` (async), `A --> B : return`, combined fragments: `alt`/`else`/`end`, `opt`, `loop`, `par`/`else`, `break`, `critical`.

<content>
1: # Contract: Sphinx Extension API (Feature 002)
2: 
3: **Date**: 2026-05-20
4: 
5: This contract is additive to [`../../001-sysml-v2-needs/contracts/sphinx-extension-api.md`](../../001-sysml-v2-needs/contracts/sphinx-extension-api.md). Existing contracts continue to hold; this document covers only the new directives and behaviors introduced in feature `002-full-sysml-diagrams`.
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## 1. Conf.py setup (unchanged)
10: 
11: Engineers using this feature need no new entries in `conf.py` beyond what 001 already requires:
12: 
13: ```python
14: extensions = [
15:     "sphinx_needs",
16:     "sphinxcontrib.plantuml",
17:     "sphinxcontrib.sysml",
18:     "sphinx_need_svg",   # optional — only needed for *-svg directives
19: ]
20: plantuml_output_format = "svg"
21: ```
22: 
23: When `sphinx_need_svg` is absent from `extensions`, the SVG companion directives (`needsysml-stm-svg`, `needsysml-act-svg`, …) are not registered. The corresponding PlantUML directives still work.
24: 
25: ---
26: 
27: ## 2. New directives (PlantUML path)
28: 
29: All new PlantUML directives follow the same surface as the existing `needsysml-bdd` / `needsysml-ibd` / `needsysml-req` directives: they wrap `.. needuml::` with a pre-baked Jinja template + a per-diagram `:config: sysml_<x>` flow config.
30: 
31: ### 2.1 `needsysml-stm` — State Machine Diagram
32: 
33: ```rst
34: .. needsysml-stm:: <statedef-id>
35:    :scale: <N>%
36:    :align: <left|center|right>
37: ```
38: 
39: **Required argument**: A `StateDef` need ID. The renderer walks all `StateUsage` needs whose `definition` equals this `StateDef`, plus all `Transition` needs whose `from_state` or `to_state` lies in that set.
40: 
41: **Optional options**:
42: - `:scale:` — PlantUML scale (e.g. `80%`).
43: - `:align:` — diagram horizontal alignment.
44: 
45: **Output**: PlantUML state diagram with composite states, history pseudostates, choice/junction, transitions labelled `trigger [guard] / effect`. Need links activate when `plantuml_output_format = "svg"`.
46: 
47: ### 2.2 `needsysml-act` — Activity Diagram
48: 
49: ```rst
50: .. needsysml-act:: <actiondef-id>
51:    :show-partitions: true|false
52:    :scale: <N>%
53:    :align: <left|center|right>
54: ```
55: 
56: **Required argument**: An `ActionDef` need ID. The renderer walks all `Action` needs whose `definition` equals this `ActionDef`, groups by `partition`, then walks `ControlFlow` and `ObjectFlow` edges among those actions.
57: 
58: **Optional options**:
59: - `:show-partitions:` — when `true` (default), emit swimlane blocks per distinct `partition` value. When `false`, all actions render at the top level.
60: - `:scale:`, `:align:` — as above.
61: 
62: **Output**: PlantUML activity-beta diagram with swimlanes, fork/join, decision/merge, and object-flow nodes.
63: 
64: ### 2.3 `needsysml-sd` — Sequence Diagram
65: 
66: ```rst
67: .. needsysml-sd:: <interaction-id>
68:    :scale: <N>%
69:    :align: <left|center|right>
70: ```
71: 
72: **Required argument**: An ID identifying the interaction. Conventionally an `ActionDef` representing the interaction; the renderer walks `Lifeline` needs whose `definition` equals this ID, plus `Message` needs whose `from_lifeline` or `to_lifeline` lies in that set.
73: 
74: **Output**: PlantUML sequence diagram with lifelines, sync/async/return messages, combined fragments grouped by `fragment_group`.
75: 
76: ### 2.4 `needsysml-uc` — Use Case Diagram
77: 
78: ```rst
79: .. needsysml-uc:: <filter-expression>
80:    :subject: <name>
81:    :scale: <N>%
82:    :align: <left|center|right>
83: ```
84: 
85: **Required argument**: A sphinx-needs filter expression selecting which use cases to draw. Default in the absence of an argument is `type == 'UseCase'`.
86: 
87: **Optional options**:
88: - `:subject:` — restrict to use cases with this `subject` value. Default: all subjects, rendered in separate boundary rectangles.
89: - `:scale:`, `:align:` — as above.
90: 
91: **Output**: PlantUML use case diagram with actor figures outside one or more system-boundary rectangles, solid association lines from each actor to the use cases listed in its `interacts_with` field, and dashed labelled arrows for the `extends` / `includes` / `generalizes` use-case-to-use-case relationships.
92: 
93: ### 2.5 `needsysml-pkg` — Package Diagram
94: 
95: ```rst
96: .. needsysml-pkg:: <root-package-id>
97:    :depth: <N>
98:    :scale: <N>%
99:    :align: <left|center|right>
100: ```
101: 
102: **Required argument**: A `Package` need ID — the root of the rendered tree.
103: 
104: **Optional options**:
105: - `:depth:` — max nesting depth (default 3).
106: - `:scale:`, `:align:` — as above.
107: 
108: **Output**: PlantUML package diagram with nested packages and labelled dependency arrows.
109: 
110: ### 2.6 `needsysml-par` (v1.1) — Parametric Diagram
111: 
112: ```rst
113: .. needsysml-par:: <constraintblock-id>
114:    :scale: <N>%
115:    :align: <left|center|right>
116: ```
117: 
118: **Required argument**: A `ConstraintBlock` need ID. The renderer draws the constraint block, its parameters, and the value properties bound to those parameters by `BindingConnector` edges.
119: 
120: **Output**: PlantUML class-diagram approximation (per `research.md § 1`) with a `<<constraint>>`-stereotyped class and labelled binding arrows.
121: 
122: ### 2.7 `needsysml-alloc` (v1.1) — Allocation Matrix
123: 
124: ```rst
125: .. needsysml-alloc::
126:    :rows: <filter-expression>
127:    :columns: <filter-expression>
128:    :marker: <character>
129: ```
130: 
131: **No required argument** (per spec Q3 — defaults work for the common case).
132: 
133: **Optional options**:
134: - `:rows:` — filter expression for the row axis. Default: `allocates != ""`.
135: - `:columns:` — filter expression for the column axis. Default: needs referenced by any `allocates` value (computed from the row set).
136: - `:marker:` — character placed at allocated intersections. Default `✓`.
137: 
138: **Output**: A docutils `<table>` with row-need links as row headers, column-need links as column headers, and the marker character at intersections.
139: 
140: ---
141: 
142: ## 3. New directives (SVG companion path)
143: 
144: For each of `stm`, `act`, `sd`, `uc`, `pkg`, `par`, plus the previously-existing `ibd` and `bdd`, register a `-svg` companion directive: `needsysml-stm-svg`, `needsysml-act-svg`, `needsysml-sd-svg`, `needsysml-uc-svg`, `needsysml-pkg-svg`, `needsysml-par-svg`, `needsysml-ibd-svg` (promotion), `needsysml-bdd-svg` (already present).
145: 
146: All companions follow the identical surface as the PlantUML directive (same required argument, same options) but route through the `sphinx_need_svg.directives.needsvg.Needsvg` placeholder:
147: 
148: ```python
149: def run(self) -> list[Any]:
150:     content = self._render_svg_template()
151:     targetid = f"needsvg-{self.env.docname}-{self.env.new_serialno('needsvg')}"
152:     self.env.needsvg_all_data.setdefault(targetid, {
153:         "docname": self.env.docname,
154:         "lineno": self.lineno,
155:         "content": content,
156:         "options": {"width": "100%", "height": "auto", "align": ..., "debug": False},
157:     })
158:     return [nodes.target("", "", ids=[targetid]),
159:             self._make_needsvg_node(targetid)]
160: ```
161: 
162: Each `-svg` directive's `_render_svg_template()` returns an SVG string with placeholder tokens (`__ROOT_ID__`, `__FILTER_EXPR__`) substituted into the Jinja-bearing template in `svg_templates.py`. Sphinx-need-svg's `process_needsvg` handler then runs the Jinja template against the live needs registry at `doctree-resolved`.
163: 
164: **Registration gate**: SVG companions are registered only when `importlib.util.find_spec("sphinx_need_svg")` succeeds. The PlantUML directives are always registered.
165: 
166: ---
167: 
168: ## 4. `needsysml-req` enhancement (additive)
169: 
170: `needsysml-req` already accepts a positional filter argument. This feature adds an optional `:filter:` option with default `type == 'Requirement'`. The positional form continues to work for backward compatibility:
171: 
172: ```rst
173: .. needsysml-req::
174:    :filter: type == 'Requirement' and status == 'accepted'
175:    :show-satisfy: true
176:    :align: center
177: ```
178: 
179: When neither the positional argument nor `:filter:` is supplied, the directive defaults to `type == 'Requirement'`.
180: 
181: ---
182: 
183: ## 5. Behavior contracts
184: 
185: ### 5.1 Unknown-reference handling (FR-016)
186: 
187: When a directive's input arguments or options reference a need ID that does not exist in the needs registry, the directive MUST:
188: 1. Emit one Sphinx warning, categorized under `[needsysml.<diagram>.unknown-*]` (see `data-model.md § Validation Rules`).
189: 2. Render output that contains an explicit `??` or `Unknown: <id>` placeholder at the position the element would have occupied.
190: 3. NOT abort the build. The build's overall return code remains 0 unless the user has enabled `-W` (warnings-as-errors).
191: 
192: ### 5.2 Renderer-not-installed handling (FR-017)
193: 
194: When `sphinx_need_svg` is not installed:
195: - `-svg` companion directives MUST NOT be registered (no `app.add_directive` call).
196: - The PlantUML directives MUST continue to register and function.
197: - Documents that include `-svg` directives produce Sphinx's standard `unknown directive` warning, not an extension crash. This is the same degradation engineers see for `sphinxcontrib-plantuml` already.
198: 
199: When `sphinxcontrib.plantuml` is not installed:
200: - The PlantUML directives ARE registered but emit a warning (existing 001 behavior).
201: - `-svg` companions, if registered, continue to work — they don't depend on PlantUML.
202: 
203: ### 5.3 Empty-result handling (FR-018)
204: 
205: When a directive's filter resolves to an empty set (no states, no actions, no packages, …):
206: - The directive MUST render an SVG/PlantUML output containing an explicit "No matching elements" placeholder.
207: - The directive MUST emit one informational warning (`[needsysml.<diagram>.empty]`) so engineers learn about the empty result without a hard failure.
208: 
209: ### 5.4 Clickable links (FR-013, FR-014)
210: 
211: - PlantUML directives: each rendered need is wrapped in PlantUML's `[[<docname>.html#<need-id> <title>]]` link syntax. Requires `plantuml_output_format = "svg"`. The existing warning in `warnings.py` continues to fire when this is not set.
212: - SVG companions: each rendered need is wrapped in an SVG `<a href="<docname>.html#<need-id>">` element produced by `sphinx_need_svg.jinja_context.SvgJinjaContext.flow()`. Works in every output format that supports inline SVG.
213: 
214: ### 5.5 Allocation matrix non-link cells (FR-013)
215: 
216: Row and column headers in the matrix link to the corresponding need anchors. Interior marker cells do NOT link.
217: 
218: ---
219: 
220: ## 6. Extension setup function
221: 
222: `sphinxcontrib.sysml.setup(app)` is extended:
223: 
224: ```python
225: def setup(app: Sphinx) -> dict[str, object]:
226:     app.setup_extension("sphinx_needs")
227:     app.connect("config-inited", _register_types_and_fields)
228:     app.connect("builder-inited", _register_flow_configs)
229:     app.connect("builder-inited", _warn_plantuml_format)
230: 
231:     # Existing 001 directives
232:     app.add_directive("needsysml-bdd", NeedsymlBddDirective)
233:     app.add_directive("needsysml-ibd", NeedsymlIbdDirective)
234:     app.add_directive("needsysml-req", NeedsymlReqDirective)
235: 
236:     # v1 new directives
237:     app.add_directive("needsysml-stm", NeedsymlStmDirective)
238:     app.add_directive("needsysml-act", NeedsymlActDirective)
239:     app.add_directive("needsysml-sd",  NeedsymlSdDirective)
240:     app.add_directive("needsysml-uc",  NeedsymlUcDirective)
241:     app.add_directive("needsysml-pkg", NeedsymlPkgDirective)
242: 
243:     # v1.1 new directives
244:     app.add_directive("needsysml-par",   NeedsymlParDirective)
245:     app.add_directive("needsysml-alloc", NeedsymlAllocDirective)
246: 
247:     if _HAS_NEED_SVG:
248:         # v0.2 svg
249:         app.add_directive("needsysml-bdd-svg", NeedsymlBddSvgDirective)
250:         # v1 svg
251:         app.add_directive("needsysml-ibd-svg", NeedsymlIbdSvgDirective)
252:         app.add_directive("needsysml-stm-svg", NeedsymlStmSvgDirective)
253:         app.add_directive("needsysml-act-svg", NeedsymlActSvgDirective)
254:         app.add_directive("needsysml-sd-svg",  NeedsymlSdSvgDirective)
255:         app.add_directive("needsysml-uc-svg",  NeedsymlUcSvgDirective)
256:         app.add_directive("needsysml-pkg-svg", NeedsymlPkgSvgDirective)
257:         # v1.1 svg
258:         app.add_directive("needsysml-par-svg", NeedsymlParSvgDirective)
259: 
260:     return {
261:         "version": VERSION,
262:         "parallel_read_safe": True,
263:         "parallel_write_safe": True,
264:     }
265: ```
266: 
267: ---
268: 
269: ## 7. Versioning
270: 
271: | Release | Version | Contents |
272: |---|---|---|
273: | v1 | `sphinxcontrib-sysml 0.3.0` | All 13 new need types, ~25 new fields, 6 new flow configs, all six v1 PlantUML directives, all six v1 SVG companions, `needsysml-req` `:filter:` option, `needsysml-ibd-svg` promotion. v1.1 element types (ConstraintBlock, ConstraintParameter, ValueProperty, BindingConnector) registered but their directives NOT yet added. |
274: | v1.1 | `sphinxcontrib-sysml 0.4.0` | `needsysml-alloc` matrix, `needsysml-par` + `needsysml-par-svg` directives. Their templates added. No new types/fields beyond v1. |
275: 
276: Bump `sphinxcontrib/sysml/__init__.py:VERSION` and `pyproject.toml:[project] version` in lockstep; `tool.commitizen.version_files` already lists both. Commit message style: `feat: full SysML diagram coverage (v1)` etc., per the project's existing commitizen config.

(End of file - total 276 lines)
</content>

# Contract: Sphinx Extension API (Feature 002)

**Date**: 2026-05-20

This contract is additive to [`../../001-sysml-v2-needs/contracts/sphinx-extension-api.md`](../../001-sysml-v2-needs/contracts/sphinx-extension-api.md). Existing contracts continue to hold; this document covers only the new directives and behaviors introduced in feature `002-full-sysml-diagrams`.

---

## 1. Conf.py setup (unchanged)

Engineers using this feature need no new entries in `conf.py` beyond what 001 already requires:

```python
extensions = [
    "sphinx_needs",
    "sphinxcontrib.plantuml",
    "sphinxcontrib.sysml",
    "sphinx_need_svg",   # optional — only needed for *-svg directives
]
plantuml_output_format = "svg"

state.output:
<path>/home/chris/develop/open_source/sphinx-need-sysml/specs/002-full-sysml-diagrams/quickstart.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # Quickstart: Full SysML v2 Diagram Coverage
2: 
3: **Date**: 2026-05-20
4: 
5: This quickstart shows an engineer the shortest path from a fresh Sphinx project to a working SysML model with every diagram type rendered. Assumes feature `002-full-sysml-diagrams` has shipped (v1 ≥ 0.3.0; v1.1 features marked).
6: 
7: ---
8: 
9: ## Install
10: 
11: ```bash
12: pip install "sphinxcontrib-sysml>=0.3.0" sphinxcontrib-plantuml sphinx-need-svg
13: ```
14: 
15: `sphinx-need-svg` is optional but unlocks the `-svg` companion directives.
16: 
17: ## conf.py
18: 
19: ```python
20: extensions = [
21:     "sphinx_needs",
22:     "sphinxcontrib.plantuml",
23:     "sphinxcontrib.sysml",
24:     "sphinx_need_svg",      # optional
25: ]
26: plantuml_output_format = "svg"
27: ```
28: 
29: No additional configuration is required — the extension auto-registers all 27 need types and all flow configs at setup time.
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## Five-minute model walkthrough
34: 
35: The vehicle example in `docs/examples/vehicle_system.rst` exercises every diagram. The snippets below show one section per diagram — enough to render a working model.
36: 
37: ### 1. State machine (v1)
38: 
39: ```rst
40: .. statedef:: EngineState
41:    :id: SD-001
42: 
43:    Lifecycle of the engine.
44: 
45: .. stateusage:: off
46:    :id: SU-001
47:    :definition: SD-001
48:    :pseudo_kind: initial
49: 
50: .. stateusage:: starting
51:    :id: SU-002
52:    :definition: SD-001
53: 
54: .. stateusage:: running
55:    :id: SU-003
56:    :definition: SD-001
57: 
58: .. stateusage:: stopping
59:    :id: SU-004
60:    :definition: SD-001
61: 
62: .. transition:: key_on
63:    :id: TRANS-001

143: 
144: ### 3. Sequence (v1)

146: ```rst

164:    :definition: AD-010
165:    :represents: PD-011
166: 
167: .. message:: turn_key
168:    :id: MSG-001
169:    :from_lifeline: LIFELINE-001
170:    :to_lifeline: LIFELINE-002
171:    :message_kind: sync
172: 
173: .. message:: crank
174:    :id: MSG-002
175:    :from_lifeline: LIFELINE-002
176:    :to_lifeline: LIFELINE-003
177:    :message_kind: async
178:    :fragment_group: F1
179:    :fragment_kind: alt
180:    :fragment_guard: key_position == "start"
181: 
182: .. message:: ok
183:    :id: MSG-003
184:    :from_lifeline: LIFELINE-003
185:    :to_lifeline: LIFELINE-002
186:    :message_kind: return
187:    :fragment_group: F1

190:    :align: center
191:

193:    :align: center
194: ```
195: 
196: ### 4. Use case (v1)
197: 
198: ```rst
199: .. actor:: Driver
200:    :id: ACTOR-001
201:    :interacts_with: USECASE-001
202: 
203: .. actor:: Mechanic
204:    :id: ACTOR-002
205:    :interacts_with: USECASE-002, USECASE-003
206: 
207: .. usecase:: Start engine
208:    :id: USECASE-001
209:    :subject: Vehicle
210: 
211: .. usecase:: Diagnose fault
212:    :id: USECASE-002
213:    :subject: Vehicle
214:    :extends: USECASE-001
215: 
216: .. usecase:: Authenticate key
217:    :id: USECASE-003
218:    :subject: Vehicle
219: 
220: .. needsysml-uc:: type == 'UseCase'
221:    :subject: Vehicle
222:    :align: center
223: 
224: .. needsysml-uc-svg:: type == 'UseCase'
225:    :subject: Vehicle
226:    :align: center
227: ```
228: 
229: ### 5. Package (v1)
230: 
231: ```rst
232: .. package:: VehicleSystem
233:    :id: PKG-001
234: 
235: .. package:: Powertrain
236:    :id: PKG-002
237:    :parent_package: PKG-001
238: 
239: .. package:: Chassis
240:    :id: PKG-003
241:    :parent_package: PKG-001
242: 
243: .. dependency::
244:    :id: DEP-001
245:    :source_ref: PKG-002
246:    :target_ref: PKG-003
247:    :kind: use
248: 
249: .. needsysml-pkg:: PKG-001
250:    :depth: 3
251:    :align: center
252: 
253: .. needsysml-pkg-svg:: PKG-001
254:    :align: center
255: ```
256: 
257: ### 6. Allocation matrix (v1.1)
258: 
259: ```rst
260: .. needsysml-alloc::
261: 
262: .. needsysml-alloc::
263:    :rows: type == 'Action'
264:    :columns: type == 'Part'
265: ```
266: 
267: The first invocation uses the defaults from spec Q3 (rows = needs with non-empty `allocates`, columns = needs referenced by those allocations). The second invocation explicitly maps actions to parts.
268: 
269: ### 7. Parametric (v1.1)
270: 
271: ```rst
272: .. constraintparameter:: output_param
273:    :id: PARAM-001
274: 
275: .. constraintparameter:: duration_param
276:    :id: PARAM-002
277: 
278: .. constraintparameter:: efficiency_param
279:    :id: PARAM-003
280: 
281: .. constraintparameter:: fuel_param
282:    :id: PARAM-004
283: 
284: .. constraintblock:: FuelConsumption
285:    :id: CONSTRAINT-001
286:    :parameters: PARAM-001, PARAM-002, PARAM-003, PARAM-004
287:    :expression: fuel = output * duration / efficiency
288: 
289: .. valueproperty:: engine_output
290:    :id: VALUE-001
291:    :owned_by: P-002
292:    :value_type: kW
293:    :default_value: "150"
294: 
295: .. valueproperty:: trip_duration
296:    :id: VALUE-002
297:    :owned_by: P-001
298:    :value_type: s
299: 
300: .. bindingconnector::
301:    :id: BIND-001
302:    :source_parameter: PARAM-001
303:    :target_value: VALUE-001
304:    :unit: kW
305: 
306: .. bindingconnector::
307:    :id: BIND-002
308:    :source_parameter: PARAM-002
309:    :target_value: VALUE-002
310:    :unit: s
311: 
312: .. needsysml-par:: CONSTRAINT-001
313:    :align: center
314: 
315: .. needsysml-par-svg:: CONSTRAINT-001
316:    :align: center
317: ```
318: 
319: ---
320: 
321: ## Build and verify
322: 
323: ```bash
324: sphinx-build -W docs docs/_build/html
325: ```
326: 
327: The `-W` strict mode catches missing references and empty diagrams. Per spec edge cases, an unknown `from_state` reference produces one categorized warning and a `??` placeholder on the diagram rather than a build failure under default settings; with `-W` the warning becomes an error.
328: 
329: Browse `docs/_build/html/examples/vehicle_system.html` and click any state, action, lifeline, package, or actor box — it navigates to the need's definition anchor on the page.
330: 
331: ---
332: 
333: ## Troubleshooting
334: 
335: - **"Unknown directive `needsysml-stm-svg`"** — `sphinx-need-svg` is not installed or not in `extensions`. The PlantUML `needsysml-stm` still works without it.
336: - **PlantUML diagram links don't navigate** — `plantuml_output_format` is not `svg`. Set it in `conf.py`.
337: - **Build hangs at `pickling environment...`** — large activity diagrams with many partitions and flows may take seconds to render. SC-008 budgets the full vehicle example at < 30 s.
338: - **State diagram shows ordinary boxes instead of pseudostates** — your `StateUsage` is missing `:pseudo_kind:`. If you supplied an unrecognized value, sphinx-needs rejects it at parse time with a schema-validation error (the `pseudo_kind` field is enum-constrained); fix the value to one of `initial`, `final`, `shallowHistory`, `deepHistory`, `choice`, `junction`.
339: - **`needsysml-act` skips a swimlane** — verify each `Action`'s `:partition:` is spelled consistently across the activity. `partition: "Driver"` and `partition: "driver"` group into different lanes.
340: 
341: ---
342: 
343: ## v1 verification results (measured 2026-05-21)
344: 
345: Recorded against the full vehicle example with all v1 diagrams (bdd, ibd, req, stm, act, sd, uc, pkg) rendered side-by-side in PlantUML and SVG.
346: 
347: | Success criterion | Target | Measured |
348: |---|---|---|
349: | SC-003 Strict docs build is clean | 0 warnings | ✅ 0 warnings |
350: | SC-004 Drawn nodes link to need anchors | 100% | ✅ 78 unique need-anchor `href` links in `vehicle_system.html` |
351: | SC-008 Vehicle example build wall-clock | < 30 s | ✅ 10.5 s on dev workstation |
352: 

(End of file - total 352 lines)
</content>

# Quickstart: Full SysML v2 Diagram Coverage

**Date**: 2026-05-20

This quickstart shows an engineer the shortest path from a fresh Sphinx project to a working SysML model with every diagram type rendered. Assumes feature `002-full-sysml-diagrams` has shipped (v1 ≥ 0.3.0; v1.1 features marked).

---

## Install

```bash
pip install "sphinxcontrib-sysml>=0.3.0" sphinxcontrib-plantuml sphinx-need-svg
```

`sphinx-need-svg` is optional but unlocks the `-svg` companion directives.

## conf.py

from sphinxcontrib.sysml.fields import SYSML_FIELDS

VERSION = "0.3.0"

# Detect sphinx-need-svg availability

2: 
3: import logging
4: 
5: from sphinx.application import Sphinx
6: 
7: _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED = False
8: 
9: 
10: def warn_plantuml_format(app: Sphinx) -> None:
11:     """Emit a Sphinx warning if plantuml_output_format is not 'svg'.
12:

14:     """
15:     global _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED
16:     if _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED:
17:         return
18:

20:     if fmt != "svg":
21:         _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED = True
22:         _get_logger().warning(
23:             "sphinxcontrib-sysml: PlantUML hyperlinks require "
24:             "plantuml_output_format = 'svg'. Set this in conf.py for clickable diagrams."
25:         )
26: 
27: 
28: def _get_logger() -> logging.Logger:
29:     from sphinx.util import logging as sphinx_logging
30: 
31:     return sphinx_logging.getLogger(__name__)  # type: ignore[return-value]
32: 
33: 
34: # === Shared helpers for FR-016 + FR-018 ===
35: # Every needsysml-* directive uses these so the warning categories and
36: # placeholder text are uniform across diagrams.
37: 
38: 
39: def warn_unknown_ref(
40:     app: Sphinx,
41:     docname: str,
42:     lineno: int,
43:     diagram: str,
44:     ref_kind: str,
45:     ref_id: str,
46: ) -> str:
47:     """Emit a Sphinx warning for an unknown reference and return a placeholder.
48: 
49:     Use when a diagram element references a need ID that does not exist
50:     in the live needs registry. Implements FR-016.
51: 
52:     Args:
53:         app: The Sphinx application.
54:         docname: The document name where the reference appears.
55:         lineno: The source line number of the referencing directive.
56:         diagram: The diagram family (e.g. ``"stm"``, ``"act"``, ``"sd"``).
57:         ref_kind: The kind of reference (e.g. ``"state"``, ``"action"``,
58:             ``"lifeline"``, ``"target"``, ``"binding"``).
59:         ref_id: The unresolved need ID.
60: 
61:     Returns:
62:         A short placeholder string (``"?? <ref_id>"``) the caller should
63:         substitute at the element's position in the rendered diagram.
64:     """
65:     category = f"needsysml.{diagram}.unknown-{ref_kind}"
66:     location = f"{docname}:{lineno}"
67:     _get_logger().warning(
68:         "%s: unknown %s reference '%s' — rendering placeholder",
69:         location,
70:         ref_kind,
71:         ref_id,
72:         type=category,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
73:     )
74:     return f"?? {ref_id}"
75: 
76: 
77: def warn_empty(
78:     app: Sphinx,
79:     docname: str,
80:     lineno: int,
81:     diagram: str,
82: ) -> str:
83:     """Emit a Sphinx info-warning for an empty diagram and return a placeholder.
84: 
85:     Use when a diagram's element filter resolves to zero matches.
86:     Implements FR-018.
87: 
88:     Args:
89:         app: The Sphinx application.
90:         docname: The document name where the directive appears.
91:         lineno: The source line number of the directive.
92:         diagram: The diagram family (e.g. ``"stm"``, ``"act"``).
93: 
94:     Returns:
95:         A short placeholder string the caller should display at the
96:         position the empty content would have occupied.
97:     """
98:     category = f"needsysml.{diagram}.empty"
99:     location = f"{docname}:{lineno}"
100:     _get_logger().warning(
101:         "%s: no matching elements for %s diagram — rendering placeholder",
102:         location,
103:         diagram,
104:         type=category,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
105:     )
106:     return "No matching elements"

import logging

from sphinx.application import Sphinx

_PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED = False


def warn_plantuml_format(app: Sphinx) -> None:
    """Emit a Sphinx warning if plantuml_output_format is not 'svg'.

    Only emits once per build to avoid noise.
    """
    global _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED
    if _PLANTUML_WARNING_EMITTED:
        return

    fmt = getattr(app.config, "plantuml_output_format", None)
    if fmt != "svg":

265: {% endfor %}

267: {% endif %}
268: {% endfor %}
269: {% else %}
270: {% for a in actions %}
271: class "{{ a.id }}\\n{{ a.title }}" <<{{ a.activity_kind or 'action' }}>> [[{{ ref(a.id) }}]]
272:

274: {% endif %}
275: {% for f in ctrl_flows %}
276: {% if f.from_action in action_ids and f.to_action in action_ids %}
277: "{{ f.from_action }}\\n{{ needs.get(f.from_action).title }}" --> "{{ f.to_action }}\\n{{ needs.get(f.to_action).title }}"
278: {% endif %}
279: {% endfor %}
280: {% for f in obj_flows %}
281: {% if f.from_action in action_ids and f.to_action in action_ids %}
282: "{{ f.from_action }}\\n{{ needs.get(f.from_action).title }}" --> "{{ f.to_action }}\\n{{ needs.get(f.to_action).title }}" : {{ f.object_type or 'object' }}
283: {% endif %}
284: {% endfor %}
285: @enduml
286: """

289: # State Machine — walks all StateUsage instances of the root StateDef plus
290: # their Transitions, emits PlantUML state-diagram syntax. Pseudostates are
291: # rendered with their UML notation (initial/final via [*], history via
292: # <<history>>, choice via <<choice>>, junction via <<junction>>).
293: STM_FULL_TEMPLATE = """\

295: {% set root_id = "{root_id}" %}
296: {% set states = filter("type == 'StateUsage' and definition == '" + root_id + "'") | list %}
297: {% set transitions = filter("type == 'Transition'") | list %}

302: {% elif s.pseudo_kind == 'junction' %}
303: state {{ s.id }} <<junction>>
304: {% elif s.pseudo_kind == 'shallowHistory' %}
305: state {{ s.id }} <<history>>
306: {% elif s.pseudo_kind == 'deepHistory' %}
307: state {{ s.id }} <<history*>>
308: {% elif s.pseudo_kind not in ('initial', 'final') %}
309: state "{{ s.title }}" as {{ s.id }} [[{{ ref(s.id) }}]]
310: {% endif %}
311: {% endfor %}
312: {% for t in transitions %}
313: {% if t.from_state in state_ids and t.to_state in state_ids %}
314: {% set src = needs.get(t.from_state) %}
315: {% set dst = needs.get(t.to_state) %}
316: {% set src_render = '[*]' if (src and src.pseudo_kind == 'initial') else t.from_state %}

318: {% set label = t.trigger or '' %}

321: {{ src_render }} --> {{ dst_render }}{% if label %} : {{ label }}{% endif %}

323: {% endif %}

"""SVG Jinja templates for the `needsysml-*-svg` directives.

Each constant is a Jinja2 template string rendered by sphinx-need-svg's
``SvgJinjaContext`` (which exposes ``needs``, ``filter``, ``flow``, and
``ref`` to the template). Directives substitute placeholder tokens like
``__ROOT_ID__`` or ``__FILTER_EXPR__`` into the template before handing
it off to the ``Needsvg`` placeholder pipeline for deferred rendering at
``doctree-resolved``.
"""

__all__ = [
    "ACT_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "BDD_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "IBD_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "PKG_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "SD_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "STM_SVG_TEMPLATE",
    "UC_SVG_TEMPLATE",
]

"""Needsyml BDD directive — wraps needuml with pre-baked BDD template."""

from typing import Any

from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
from docutils.statemachine import StringList
from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective

from sphinxcontrib.sysml.templates import BDD_FULL_TEMPLATE


class NeedsymlBddDirective(SphinxDirective):
    """Generate a Block Definition Diagram for a given PartDef need.

    Wraps ``.. needuml::`` with ``:config: sysml_bdd`` and the
    ``BDD_FULL_TEMPLATE`` body.
    """

    required_arguments = 1

1: """Needsyml SVG directives — render diagrams as inline SVG via sphinx-need-svg.
2: 
3: We build a pre-baked Jinja template (root, children-from-filter, layout)
4: and hand it off to sphinx-need-svg's ``Needsvg`` placeholder node so that
5: rendering happens at ``doctree-resolved`` time. Doing the render in our
6: own ``run()`` would call ``SphinxNeedsData.get_needs_view()`` mid-parse,
7: which freezes the needs registry and breaks every later directive.
8: 
9: Hosts the BDD-svg directive (existing) and the IBD-svg directive
10: (promotion of the raw-needsvg workaround that used to live in
11: ``docs/examples/vehicle_system.rst``).
12: """
13: 
14: from typing import Any
15: 
16: from docutils import nodes
17: from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
18: from sphinx.application import Sphinx
19: from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective
20: 
21: from sphinxcontrib.sysml.svg_templates import (
22:     BDD_SVG_TEMPLATE,
23:     IBD_SVG_TEMPLATE,
24: )
25: 
26: 
27: def _emit_needsvg_node(
28:     directive: SphinxDirective,
29:     content: str,
30:     width: str,
31:     align: str,
32: ) -> list[Any]:
33:     """Stash the template into env.needsvg_all_data and return placeholder nodes.
34: 
35:     Shared by every ``needsysml-*-svg`` directive — keeps the deferred
36:     rendering plumbing in one place.
37:     """
38:     from sphinx_need_svg.directives.needsvg import Needsvg
39: 
40:     env = directive.env
41:     serial = env.new_serialno("needsvg")
42:     targetid = f"needsvg-{env.docname}-{serial}"
43: 
44:     if not hasattr(env, "needsvg_all_data"):
45:         env.needsvg_all_data = {}  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
46: 
47:     env.needsvg_all_data[targetid] = {  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
48:         "docname": env.docname,
49:         "lineno": directive.lineno,
50:         "content": content,
51:         "options": {
52:             "width": width,
53:             "height": "auto",
54:             "align": align,
55:             "debug": False,
56:         },
57:     }
58: 
59:     targetnode = nodes.target("", "", ids=[targetid])
60:     node = Needsvg("")
61:     node["targetid"] = targetid
62:     return [targetnode, node]
63: 
64: 
65: def _substitute(template: str, root_id: str, filter_expr: str | None = None) -> str:
66:     """Substitute the two placeholder tokens into an SVG Jinja template."""
67:     out = template.replace("__ROOT_ID__", root_id)
68:     if filter_expr is not None:
69:         out = out.replace("__FILTER_EXPR__", filter_expr.replace('"', '\\"'))
70:     return out
71: 
72: 
73: class NeedsymlBddSvgDirective(SphinxDirective):
74:     """Generate a Block Definition Diagram as inline SVG."""
75: 
76:     required_arguments = 1
77:     optional_arguments = 0
78:     final_argument_whitespace = True
79:     option_spec = {
80:         "depth": directives.unchanged,
81:         "filter": directives.unchanged,
82:         "align": directives.unchanged,
83:         "width": directives.unchanged,
84:     }
85: 
86:     def run(self) -> list[Any]:
87:         root_id = self.arguments[0]
88:         align = self.options.get("align", "center")
89:         width = self.options.get("width", "100%")
90:         filter_expr = self.options.get(
91:             "filter", f"type == 'partdef' and owned_by == '{root_id}'"
92:         )
93:         content = _substitute(BDD_SVG_TEMPLATE, root_id, filter_expr)
94:         return _emit_needsvg_node(self, content, width, align)
95: 
96: 
97: class NeedsymlIbdSvgDirective(SphinxDirective):
98:     """Generate an Internal Block Diagram as inline SVG.
99: 
100:     Promotion of the raw-needsvg IBD template that previously lived in
101:     ``docs/examples/vehicle_system.rst``. Renders the root PartDef's owned
102:     Parts with their owned Ports inside a dashed system-boundary rectangle.
103:     """
104: 
105:     required_arguments = 1
106:     optional_arguments = 0
107:     final_argument_whitespace = True
108:     option_spec = {
109:         "show-ports": directives.unchanged,
110:         "align": directives.unchanged,
111:         "width": directives.unchanged,
112:     }
113: 
114:     def run(self) -> list[Any]:
115:         root_id = self.arguments[0]
116:         align = self.options.get("align", "center")
117:         width = self.options.get("width", "100%")
118:         content = _substitute(IBD_SVG_TEMPLATE, root_id)
119:         return _emit_needsvg_node(self, content, width, align)
120: 
121: 
122: def setup(app: Sphinx) -> None:
123:     """Register the SVG directives (BDD + IBD)."""
124:     app.add_directive("needsysml-bdd-svg", NeedsymlBddSvgDirective)
125:     app.add_directive("needsysml-ibd-svg", NeedsymlIbdSvgDirective)

"""Needsyml SVG directives — render diagrams as inline SVG via sphinx-need-svg.

We build a pre-baked Jinja template (root, children-from-filter, layout)
and hand it off to sphinx-need-svg's ``Needsvg`` placeholder node so that
rendering happens at ``doctree-resolved`` time. Doing the render in our
own ``run()`` would call ``SphinxNeedsData.get_needs_view()`` mid-parse,
which freezes the needs registry and breaks every later directive.

Hosts the BDD-svg directive (existing) and the IBD-svg directive
(promotion of the raw-needsvg workaround that used to live in
``docs/examples/vehicle_system.rst``).
"""

from docutils import nodes
from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective

"""Tests for the needsysml-alloc directive."""

import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


@pytest.fixture()
def alloc_app(make_app, tmp_path):
    """Build the alloc test fixture and return the Sphinx app."""
    srcdir = Path(__file__).parent / "doc_test" / "alloc"
    tmproot = tmp_path / "alloc"
    shutil.copytree(srcdir, tmproot)
    app = make_app(srcdir=tmproot)
    app.build()
    return app


class TestAllocDirective:
    """Smoke tests for the needsysml-alloc directive."""

    def test_alloc_builds_without_error(self, alloc_app):
        """The fixture builds cleanly."""
        assert alloc_app is not None

    def test_alloc_renders_table(self, alloc_app):
        """The rendered HTML contains a <table> element."""
        html = (Path(alloc_app.outdir) / "index.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        assert "<table" in html

    def test_alloc_marker_present(self, alloc_app):
        """The ✓ marker appears at expected intersections."""
        html = (Path(alloc_app.outdir) / "index.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        assert "✓" in html

    def test_alloc_unallocated_row_empty(self, alloc_app):
        """R-004 (no allocates) renders as a row with no markers."""
        html = (Path(alloc_app.outdir) / "index.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        assert "R-004" in html

    def test_alloc_needs_registered(self, alloc_app):
        """All fixture requirements and parts are registered."""
        needs = alloc_app.env._needs_all_needs  # noqa: SLF001
        for need_id in ("R-001", "R-002", "R-003", "R-004", "P-001", "P-002", "P-003"):
            assert need_id in needs, f"{need_id} not registered"

1: """Needsyml requirements diagram directive — wraps needuml with pre-baked REQ template."""
2: 
3: from typing import Any
4: 
5: from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
6: from docutils.statemachine import StringList
7: from sphinx.application import Sphinx
8: from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective
9: 
10: from sphinxcontrib.sysml.templates import REQ_FULL_TEMPLATE
11: 
12: 
13: class NeedsymlReqDirective(SphinxDirective):
14:     """Generate a requirements diagram for needs matching a filter expression.
15: 
16:     Wraps ``.. needuml::`` with ``:config: sysml_req`` and the
17:     ``REQ_FULL_TEMPLATE`` body.
18:     """
19: 
20:     required_arguments = 0
21:     optional_arguments = 1
22:     final_argument_whitespace = True
23:     option_spec = {
24:         "filter": directives.unchanged,
25:         "show-satisfy": directives.unchanged,
26:         "show-refine": directives.unchanged,
27:         "show-allocate": directives.unchanged,
28:         "scale": directives.unchanged,
29:         "align": directives.unchanged,
30:     }
31: 
32:     def run(self) -> list[Any]:
33:         # Resolution order: positional argument (legacy) → :filter: option →
34:         # default 'type == "Requirement"'.
35:         if self.arguments:
36:             filter_expr = self.arguments[0]
37:         else:
38:             filter_expr = self.options.get("filter", "type == 'Requirement'")
39:         show_satisfy = self.options.get("show-satisfy", "true").lower() == "true"
40:         show_refine = self.options.get("show-refine", "true").lower() == "true"
41:         show_allocate = self.options.get("show-allocate", "true").lower() == "true"
42:         scale = self.options.get("scale")
43:         align = self.options.get("align", "center")
44: 
45:         # Render the REQ template with context variables
46:         content = (
47:             REQ_FULL_TEMPLATE.replace("{filter_expr}", filter_expr)
48:             .replace("{show_satisfy}", str(show_satisfy).lower())
49:             .replace("{show_refine}", str(show_refine).lower())
50:             .replace("{show_allocate}", str(show_allocate).lower())
51:         )
52: 
53:         from sphinx_needs.directives.needuml import NeedumlDirective
54: 
55:         content_list = StringList(content.splitlines(), source="needsysml-req")
56:         needuml = NeedumlDirective(
57:             name="needuml",
58:             arguments=[filter_expr],
59:             options={
60:                 "config": "sysml_req",
61:                 "scale": scale or "",
62:                 "align": align,
63:             },
64:             content=content_list,
65:             lineno=self.lineno,
66:             content_offset=self.content_offset,
67:             block_text=self.block_text,
68:             state=self.state,
69:             state_machine=self.state_machine,
70:         )
71: 
72:         return needuml.run()  # type: ignore[no-any-return]
73: 
74: 
75: def setup(app: Sphinx) -> None:
76:     """Register the needsysml-req directive."""
77:     app.add_directive("needsysml-req", NeedsymlReqDirective)

(End of file - total 77 lines)

"""Needsyml requirements diagram directive — wraps needuml with pre-baked REQ template."""

from typing import Any

from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
from docutils.statemachine import StringList
from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective

So `NeedsSphinxConfig(app.config)` gives me the config object. Now let me create the allocation directive.

The approach:

3. For columns: collect all IDs referenced by `allocates` fields of row needs, then filter those through the columns filter
4. Build a docutils table with:
   - Header row: empty cell + column need refs (pending_xref)
   - Data rows: row need ref + marker/empty cells

parts.text:
For the allocation matrix, I need to create links to needs. The simplest approach is to use `nodes.reference` with the appropriate `refid` and `refuri`. Since the needs are on the same page, I can use `refid` directly.

Actually, looking at the contract more carefully (§ 5.5): "Row and column headers in the matrix link to the corresponding need anchors. Interior marker cells do NOT link."

So I need to create links for row and column headers only. The simplest approach is to use `nodes.reference` with `refid` set to the need ID.

Let me now create the allocation directive. I'll use a straightforward approach:

input.content:
"""Needsyml allocation matrix directive — renders a requirement-to-part traceability table."""

from typing import Any

from docutils import nodes
from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
from sphinx.addnodes import pending_xref
from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective
from sphinx_needs.config import NeedsSphinxConfig
from sphinx_needs.data import SphinxNeedsData
from sphinx_needs.filter_common import filter_needs


class NeedsymlAllocDirective(SphinxDirective):
    """Generate an allocation matrix table.

    Rows are needs matching the ``:rows:`` filter (default: needs with
    non-empty ``allocates``). Columns are the unique part IDs referenced
    by those rows' ``allocates`` fields, optionally filtered by
    ``:columns:``. A marker (default ``✓``) is placed at each allocated
    intersection.
    """

    required_arguments = 0
    optional_arguments = 0
    final_argument_whitespace = True
    option_spec = {
        "rows": directives.unchanged,
        "columns": directives.unchanged,
        "marker": directives.unchanged,
    }

    def run(self) -> list[Any]:
        env = self.env
        needs_data = SphinxNeedsData(env)
        all_needs = needs_data._env_needs  # noqa: SLF001
        config = NeedsSphinxConfig(self.config)

        rows_filter = self.options.get("rows", 'allocates != ""')
        columns_filter = self.options.get("columns", None)
        marker = self.options.get("marker", "✓")

        # Evaluate rows filter against all needs
        row_needs = filter_needs(
            all_needs.values(), config, rows_filter, location=self
        )

        # Collect column IDs from allocates fields of row needs
        col_ids: set[str] = set()
        for need in row_needs:
            allocates = need.get("allocates", "")
            if allocates:
                for aid in allocates.split(","):
                    aid = aid.strip()
                    if aid:
                        col_ids.add(aid)

        # Filter columns if a columns_filter is provided
        if columns_filter:
            col_needs = filter_needs(
                [all_needs[cid] for cid in col_ids if cid in all_needs],
                config,
                columns_filter,
                location=self,
            )
            col_ids = {n["id"] for n in col_needs}

        # Sort for deterministic output
        sorted_col_ids = sorted(col_ids)
        sorted_row_needs = sorted(row_needs, key=lambda n: n["id"])

        # Handle empty result
        if not sorted_row_needs or not sorted_col_ids:
            from sphinxcontrib.sysml.warnings import warn_empty

            placeholder = warn_empty(self.app, env.docname, self.lineno, "alloc")
            return [nodes.paragraph("", placeholder)]

        # Build the table
        table = nodes.table(classes=["needsysml-alloc-matrix"])
        tgroup = nodes.tgroup(cols=1 + len(sorted_col_ids))

        # Column specs: first column (row headers) wider, rest equal
        tgroup += nodes.colspec(colwidth=3)
        for _ in sorted_col_ids:
            tgroup += nodes.colspec(colwidth=1)

        # Header row
        header_row = nodes.row()
        header_row += nodes.entry("", nodes.paragraph("", ""))
        for col_id in sorted_col_ids:
            col_need = all_needs.get(col_id)
            if col_need:
                cell = nodes.entry()
                cell += _make_need_ref(col_need, env.docname)
                header_row += cell
            else:
                header_row += nodes.entry("", nodes.paragraph("", col_id))
        tgroup += nodes.thead("", header_row)

        # Body rows
        tbody = nodes.tbody()
        for row_need in sorted_row_needs:
            row = nodes.row()

            # Row header: link to the need
            row += nodes.entry("", _make_need_ref(row_need, env.docname))

            # Marker cells
            row_allocates = set()
            allocates = row_need.get("allocates", "")
            if allocates:
                for aid in allocates.split(","):
                    aid = aid.strip()
                    if aid:
                        row_allocates.add(aid)

            for col_id in sorted_col_ids:
                cell = nodes.entry()
                if col_id in row_allocates:
                    cell += nodes.paragraph("", marker)
                else:
                    cell += nodes.paragraph("", "")
                row += cell

            tbody += row

        tgroup += tbody
        table += tgroup
        table.line = self.lineno

        return [table]


def _make_need_ref(need_info: dict, docname: str) -> nodes.paragraph:
    """Create a paragraph with a pending cross-reference to the given need."""
    para = nodes.paragraph()
    ref = pending_xref(
        "",
        nodes.Text(need_info["id"]),
        refdomain="need",
        reftype="need",
        reftarget=need_info["id"],
        refexplicit=False,
        refdoc=docname,
    )
    para += ref
    return para


def setup(app: Sphinx) -> None:
    """Register the needsysml-alloc directive."""
    app.add_directive("needsysml-alloc", NeedsymlAllocDirective)

parts.text: Now let me create the needsysml_par.py directive module (T076). It follows the same pattern as the other PlantUML directives (wrapping NeedumlDirective) and its SVG companion uses the _emit_needsvg_node pattern.
Now create the parametric directive module (T076):

input.content:
"""Needsyml parametric diagram directive — wraps needuml with pre-baked PAR template."""

from typing import Any

from docutils.parsers.rst import directives
from docutils.statemachine import StringList
from sphinx.application import Sphinx
from sphinx.util.docutils import SphinxDirective

from sphinxcontrib.sysml.svg_templates import PAR_SVG_TEMPLATE
from sphinxcontrib.sysml.templates import PAR_FULL_TEMPLATE


class NeedsymlParDirective(SphinxDirective):
    """Generate a parametric diagram for a given ConstraintBlock need.

    Wraps ``.. needuml::`` with ``:config: sysml_par`` and the
    ``PAR_FULL_TEMPLATE`` body. Uses a class-diagram approximation
    since PlantUML has no native parametric mode.
    """

    required_arguments = 1
    optional_arguments = 0
    final_argument_whitespace = True
    option_spec = {
        "scale": directives.unchanged,
        "align": directives.unchanged,
    }

    def run(self) -> list[Any]:
        root_id = self.arguments[0]
        scale = self.options.get("scale")
        align = self.options.get("align", "center")

        content = PAR_FULL_TEMPLATE.replace("{root_id}", root_id)

        from sphinx_needs.directives.needuml import NeedumlDirective

        content_list = StringList(content.splitlines(), source="needsysml-par")
        needuml = NeedumlDirective(
            name="needuml",
            arguments=[root_id],
            options={
                "config": "sysml_par",
                "scale": scale or "",
                "align": align,
            },
            content=content_list,
            lineno=self.lineno,
            content_offset=self.content_offset,
            block_text=self.block_text,
            state=self.state,
            state_machine=self.state_machine,
        )

        return needuml.run()  # type: ignore[no-any-return]


class NeedsymlParSvgDirective(SphinxDirective):
    """Generate a parametric diagram as inline SVG."""

    required_arguments = 1
    optional_arguments = 0
    final_argument_whitespace = True
    option_spec = {
        "align": directives.unchanged,
        "width": directives.unchanged,
    }

    def run(self) -> list[Any]:
        from sphinxcontrib.sysml.directives.needsysml_svg import (
            _emit_needsvg_node,
            _substitute,
        )

        root_id = self.arguments[0]
        align = self.options.get("align", "center")
        width = self.options.get("width", "100%")
        content = _substitute(PAR_SVG_TEMPLATE, root_id)
        return _emit_needsvg_node(self, content, width, align)


def setup(app: Sphinx) -> None:
    """Register the needsysml-par directives."""
    app.add_directive("needsysml-par", NeedsymlParDirective)
    app.add_directive("needsysml-par-svg", NeedsymlParSvgDirective)

input.content:
"""Tests for the needsysml-par directive (PlantUML + SVG variants)."""

import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest


@pytest.fixture()
def par_app(make_app, tmp_path):
    """Build the par test fixture and return the Sphinx app."""
    srcdir = Path(__file__).parent / "doc_test" / "par"
    tmproot = tmp_path / "par"
    shutil.copytree(srcdir, tmproot)
    app = make_app(srcdir=tmproot)
    app.build()
    return app


class TestParDirective:
    """Smoke tests for the needsysml-par directive family."""

    def test_par_builds_without_error(self, par_app):
        """The fixture builds cleanly."""
        assert par_app is not None

    def test_par_root_id_in_rendered_html(self, par_app):
        """The root ConstraintBlock ID appears in the rendered HTML output."""
        html = (Path(par_app.outdir) / "index.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        assert "CONSTRAINT-001" in html

    def test_par_bindings_registered(self, par_app):
        """All three fixture binding connectors are registered."""
        needs = par_app.env._needs_all_needs  # noqa: SLF001
        for bind_id in ("BIND-001", "BIND-002", "BIND-003"):
            assert bind_id in needs, f"binding {bind_id} not registered"

    def test_par_unit_field_readable(self, par_app):
        """The unit field is readable on BIND-001."""
        needs = par_app.env._needs_all_needs  # noqa: SLF001
        bind = needs.get("BIND-001")
        assert bind is not None
        assert bind.get("unit") == "kW"

<path>/home/chris/develop/open_source/sphinx-need-sysml/docs/directives/needsysml_pkg.rst</path>

<content>
1: needsysml-pkg Directive
2: =======================
3: 
4: The ``.. needsysml-pkg::`` directive generates a Package Diagram from a
5: root ``Package`` need. A companion ``.. needsysml-pkg-svg::`` renders the
6: same diagram as inline SVG.
7: 
8: Usage
9: -----
10: 
11: .. code-block:: rst
12: 
13:    .. needsysml-pkg:: <root-package-id>
14:       :depth: <N>
15:       :scale: <N>%
16:       :align: <left|center|right>
17: 
18:    .. needsysml-pkg-svg:: <root-package-id>
19:       :align: <left|center|right>
20: 
21: Required Argument
22: ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
23: 
24: ``<root-package-id>``
25:    The ID of the root ``Package`` need. The directive walks
26:    ``parent_package`` relationships to build the nested tree.
27: 
28: Options
29: -------
30: 
31: ``:depth: <N>``
32:    Maximum nesting depth (default 3 — root + 2 levels of children).
33:    PlantUML variant only.
34: 
35: ``:scale: <N>%``
36:    PlantUML variant only.
37: 
38: ``:align: <left|center|right>``
39:    Horizontal alignment. Default ``center``.
40: 
41: Nesting
42: -------
43: 
44: A ``Package`` need with a ``:parent_package: <id>`` field renders inside
45: its parent's rectangle in the diagram.
46: 
47: Dependencies
48: ------------
49: 
50: A ``Dependency`` need wires two packages together:
51: 
52: .. code-block:: rst
53: 
54:    .. dependency:: pt_uses_chassis
55:       :id: DEP-001
56:       :source_ref: PKG-002
57:       :target_ref: PKG-003
58:       :kind: use
59: 
60: The ``:kind:`` enum values:
61: 
62: .. list-table::
63:    :header-rows: 1
64:    :widths: 20 80
65: 
66:    * - Value
67:      - Stereotype label
68:    * - ``use``
69:      - ``<<use>>`` (default)
70:    * - ``import``
71:      - ``<<import>>``
72:    * - ``realize``
73:      - ``<<realize>>``
74: 
75: How It Works
76: ------------
77: 
78: The PlantUML directive wraps ``.. needuml::`` with ``:config:
79: sysml_pkg`` and the ``PKG_FULL_TEMPLATE`` body. The template renders
80: the root package plus two levels of nested children (``depth=3``
81: covers most system organizations) and then emits one dashed arrow per
82: ``Dependency`` with its kind stereotype label.
83: 
84: The SVG companion (``PKG_SVG_TEMPLATE``) lays out the root rectangle
85: with its direct children inside as smaller rectangles arranged
86: horizontally, and draws labelled dashed arrows for each dependency.
87: 
88: .. note::
89: 
90:    The template is unrolled rather than recursive because the
91:    minijinja engine that ``needuml`` uses does not support
92:    ``list.append()`` — needed for recursive macros. For deeper nesting
93:    you can compose multiple ``needsysml-pkg`` invocations, each rooted
94:    at a different parent.
95: 
96: Clickable Links
97: ---------------
98: 
99: PlantUML variant: each ``package`` carries a ``[[<docname>.html#<id>]]``
100: link active when ``plantuml_output_format = "svg"``. SVG variant: each
101: package rectangle is wrapped in a native ``<a href>``.

(End of file - total 101 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
needsysml-pkg Directive
=======================

The ``.. needsysml-pkg::`` directive generates a Package Diagram from a
root ``Package`` need. A companion ``.. needsysml-pkg-svg::`` renders the
same diagram as inline SVG.

Usage
-----

.. code-block:: rst

   .. needsysml-pkg:: <root-package-id>
      :depth: <N>
      :scale: <N>%
      :align: <left|center|right>

   .. needsysml-pkg-svg:: <root-package-id>
      :align: <left|center|right>

input.content:
needsysml-alloc Directive
=========================

The ``.. needsysml-alloc::`` directive generates an allocation matrix
table showing which requirements allocate to which parts. Unlike other
diagram directives, it renders a docutils ``<table>`` (not PlantUML or
SVG), so it works in HTML, PDF, and ePub outputs.

Usage
-----

.. code-block:: rst

   .. needsysml-alloc::
      :rows: <filter-expression>
      :columns: <filter-expression>
      :marker: <character>

No required argument — the defaults work for the common case.

Options
-------

``:rows: <filter-expression>``
   Filter expression for the row axis. Default: needs with non-empty
   ``allocates`` field (``allocates != ""``).

``:columns: <filter-expression>``
   Filter expression for the column axis. Default: all unique part IDs
   referenced by the row needs' ``allocates`` fields.

``:marker: <character>``
   Character placed at allocated intersections. Default ``✓``.

How It Works
------------

The directive evaluates the ``:rows:`` filter against all registered
needs, then collects the unique IDs referenced by each row need's
``allocates`` field to form the columns. If ``:columns:`` is provided,
those column IDs are further filtered.

Row and column headers are cross-references to the corresponding need
anchors. Interior cells contain the marker character at allocated
intersections and are empty otherwise (no links in interior cells per
FR-013).

Example
-------

.. code-block:: rst

   .. requirement:: Engine Control
      :id: R-001
      :allocates: P-001, P-002

      The engine control system shall manage fuel delivery.

   .. part:: ECU
      :id: P-001

   .. part:: Fuel Injector
      :id: P-002

   .. needsysml-alloc::

Custom filters:

.. code-block:: rst

   .. needsysml-alloc::
      :rows: type == 'Action'
      :columns: type == 'Part'

Empty Result
------------

When no needs match the rows filter, the directive emits an
informational warning (``[needsysml.alloc.empty]``) and renders a
"No matching elements" placeholder.
